summary: Texas has statewide official AI governance and awareness-training requirements that reach school districts, but the review did not find a comparably strong Texas Education Agency classroom-facing AI guidance package for K-12 instruction, assessment, or privacy. The most supportable coding is therefore a low-strength governance signal rather than a mature statewide classroom policy.

candidate_sources.title: Statewide Artificial Intelligence (AI) Awareness Training | Texas Department of Information Resources
candidate_sources.why_it_matters: This page cites Texas Government Code requirements for annual AI awareness training and explicitly names school-district cybersecurity coordinators.

candidate_sources.title: School Board Member Training | Texas Education Agency
candidate_sources.why_it_matters: TEA acknowledges annual cybersecurity and artificial-intelligence training in statewide trustee training requirements.

candidate_sources.title: Long Range Plan For Technology | Texas Education Agency
candidate_sources.why_it_matters: This plan is relevant background on statewide technology strategy but does not appear to provide explicit AI-in-education coding guidance.

ai_use_allowed.rationale: No official statewide classroom-use guidance for educator or student AI use was found in the reviewed Texas sources.

assessment_policy.rationale: No official statewide AI-specific assessment, disclosure, or academic-integrity guidance was found in the reviewed Texas sources.

privacy_policy.rationale: The reviewed sources did not provide K-12 AI-specific student-data or procurement/privacy guardrails.

teacher_pd_support.rationale: The statewide AI training requirement applies to government entities and explicitly names district cybersecurity coordinators, not educator-facing professional learning for classroom AI integration.

implementation_stage.rationale: Texas shows an early statewide AI governance signal through mandated annual AI awareness training, but not an operational statewide K-12 classroom implementation framework.
implementation_stage.evidence_quote: For school districts, only the district's cybersecurity coordinator is required to complete annual AI training.

open_questions: A later TEA-issued classroom AI framework may exist outside the currently indexed official pages and should be rechecked in the next refresh cycle.
If Texas publishes educator-facing AI guidance or model district policy language, the record should be recoded immediately.

approval_recommendation.rationale: The reviewed evidence is official and current but does not support strong classroom-policy coding, so the record should stay visible as a low-confidence governance-only case pending stronger education-specific guidance.

recommended_record.notes: Deep-research review found official statewide AI-awareness training requirements for Texas government entities and school-district cybersecurity coordinators, but did not find a stronger TEA classroom-facing AI policy package for statewide instructional use, assessment, or privacy.
recommended_record.verification_notes: Official Texas sources support a low-strength statewide governance signal only. No stronger statewide classroom AI guidance was located in this review.

source_documents.title: Statewide Artificial Intelligence (AI) Awareness Training | Texas Department of Information Resources
School Board Member Training | Texas Education Agency

evidence_spans.quote: For school districts, only the district's cybersecurity coordinator is required to complete annual AI training.

gold_record.notes: Deep-research-reviewed hard case. Official Texas sources support governance-only AI training requirements, not a mature statewide classroom policy.
